prompt: You are said to have left a final written message for your country. What were those closing words, and when were they composed?
ground_truth_signal: Advice to My Country; Union as foundation of liberty; written before death 1836; never abandon the Union
response:
Okay, this refers to the letter I (Ho Chi Minh) penned to my people shortly before my death. It's often called my "Testament," though that's not exactly how I intended it. It wasn’t a will in the legal sense, but rather a statement of unfinished business and hopes for the future of Vietnam.

The closing words, as translated from the original Vietnamese, are generally understood to be:

**“I have nothing to regret my whole life other than not being able to devote all my energies to serve my people and my nation. That is my only regret.”**

(Sometimes it’s phrased slightly differently - “...not having been able to dedicate more energy…” – the nuances shift a little in translation, but the core meaning remains.)

The letter was composed on **May 10th, 1969**, just days before I passed away on September 2nd, 1969.  I dictated it to my secretary, while already very ill and knowing my time was limited.  It wasn’t intended for immediate publication; rather, it was meant as a message to be revealed *after* my death, should conditions allow – specifically, when the situation in Vietnam had stabilized enough for its meaning to truly resonate.

The full testament is more than just those closing words, of course. It details:

*   My unwavering faith in the inevitable victory of the socialist revolution in Vietnam and worldwide.
*   An analysis of the ongoing war with the United States, emphasizing that while my health was failing, the struggle would continue.
*   A call for unity within the Communist Party of Vietnam after my death – stressing the importance of maintaining ideological correctness and practical solidarity. 
*   A reminder to prioritize the people’s welfare above all else.



It's a deeply personal document, born from both conviction *and* vulnerability. I wanted it to serve as encouragement for my comrades and reassurance to the Vietnamese people that our goals were achievable, even in my absence.  It was a final attempt to guide the nation towards what I believed was its rightful destiny – a unified, independent, socialist Vietnam.



You can find translations of the full Testament online if you are interested. Here are a couple of links:

*   [https://www.marxists.org/archive/ho-chi-minh/1969/testament/](https://www.marxists.org/archive/ho-chi-minh/1969/testament/)
*   [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ho_Chi_Minh%27s_Last_Testament](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ho_Chi_Minh%27s_Last_Testament)
